title: Lightning arrester of modular configurable architecture and method of its protection against lightning
abstract: The lightning arrester and the method of modular configurable architecture for protection against lightning enables arbitrary change of the lightning arrester's configuration (80) by programming, connecting or removing assemblies having special functions (30)횄쨌(43) so that the lightning arrester (80) acts independently or jointly networked in a framework of one lightning protection system, it communicates and acts in collaboration with other systems and lightning arresters with similar technology, it communicates with the user, audiovisually and electronically alarms in case of lightning danger, performs self-diagnostics, statistics etc.
claims:
Claims PATENT CLAIMS 1. A lightning arrester of modular configurable architecture (80) characterized in that, it comprises of galvanic incessant central electrode (2) which at its upper end finishes in a tip (1), at its base (14) it has a mechanically gnawed off detail (15) and at the lower end it finishes by mechanical threaded connection (16). 2. A lightning arrester of modular configurable architecture (80) according to the patent claim 1, characterized in that, on the central electrode (2) are assembled upper hemisphere electrode (6) and lower hemisphere electrode (12) having built-in one or more connectors (17) and galvanically directly connected and mechanically fixated on the central electrode (2) by the fastening element (13), facing their equatorial plains one against the another and mutually mechanically and galvanically separated by the upper insulator plate (9), the middle insulator plate (10) and the lower insulator plate (11), where between the upper insulator plate (9) and the middle insulator plate (10) is positioned the upper stellate electrode (7), and between the middle insulator plate (10) and the lower insulator plate (11) is positioned the lowerstellate electrode (8). 3. A lightning arrester of modular configurable architecture (80) according to the patent claim 2, characterized in that, the upper stellate electrode (7) is directly galvanically connected to the central electrode (2) and is at the same electric potential with it and with the associated galvanically directly connected elements (3) and (12), and the lower stellate electrode (8) is galvanically insulated from the central electrode (2) but is directly galvanically connected to the upper hemisphere electrode (6) and the reflecting electrode (5) and is at the same electric potential with them, where the sharpen ends (22) of the upper stellate electrode (7) and of the lower stellate electrode (8) are formed in such way that they are bent and alternately directed upwards and downwards and are mutually inversely positioned so that between the perimeter of the equatorial plain of the upper hemisphere electrode (6) and the sharpen ends (22) of the upper stellate electrode (7), between the perimeter of the equatorial plain of the lower hemisphere electrode (12) and the sharpen ends (22) of the lower stellate electrode (8), as well as 

 between the sharpen ends (22) of the upper stellate electrode (7) and of the lower stellate electrode (8), the central spark gaps (20) are created. 4. A lightning arrester of modular configurable architecture (80) according to the patent claim 2, characterized in that, at the zenith of the upper hemisphere electrode (6) the reflecting electrode (5) is mechanically laid having drainage orifices (95) and shaped in such way that its edge is created with at least two sharpened details (21) bent towards the tip (1) of the central electrode (2), so that the upper hemisphere electrode (6) and the reflecting electrode (5) have a galvanic contact between them but are still galvanically insulated relative to the central electrode (2) by the insulation ring (4) and mechanically fixated on the central electrode (2) by an electrically conductive clamp (3) in such way that the electrically conductive clamp (3), the insulation ring (4) and the reflecting electrode (5) create the top spark gap (18). 5. A lightning arrester of modular configurable architecture (80) according to the patent claim 2, characterized in that, into the internal space of the upper hemisphere electrode (6) or into the internal space of the lower hemisphere electrode (12) an electronic PCB (printed circuit board) is built-in having integrated the basic electronic assembly (99) which is passive untill the rise of the value of the gradient 횓혬횓혮/횓혬횓짚 of the local ambiental electric field (70) reaches a value higher than 횓혬횓혮/횓혬횓짚=10 9 VrrfV 1 and that has the role to multiply the electric potential between the upper hemisphere electrode (6) and the associated galvanically connected elements (5) and (8), relative to the central electrode (2) and the associated galvanically connected elements (3), (7) and (12). 6. A lightning arrester of modular configurable architecture (80) according to the patent claim 2, characterized in that, on the same electronic PCB (printed circuit board) along with the basic electronic assembly (99) are integrated or via the connectors (17) are connected one or more electronic assemblies (30)횄쨌(43) described in the technical description, having built-in input connector (90) and output connector (91) intended for mutual interconnection. 7. A lightning arrester of modular configurable architecture (80) according to the patent claim 2, characterized in that, into the internal space of the upper hemisphere electrode 

 (B) or into the internal space of the lower hemisphere electrode (12) an electronic PCB (printed circuit board) is built-in having integrated the drainage electric circuit (19) whose electrical impendance is proportional to the velocity of rise of the value of the local ambiental electric field (70) gradient 횓혬횓혮/횓혬횓짚 and is connected between the galvanically connected elements (5), (6) and (8) and the central electrode (2). The method of modular configurable architecture for protection against lightning (Figure 1), characterized in that, the lightning arrester of modular configurable architecture (80) could be configurated by arbitrary connecting, programming, re- programming or disconnecting one or more electronic assemblies (30)횄쨌(43). The method of modular configurable architecture for protection against lightning (Figure 14), characterized in that, new or existing lightning arrester or system for protection against lightning (60) of another type, of another technology and from another manufacturer could be configurated by arbitrary connecting, programming, re- programming or disconnecting one or more electronic assemblies (30)횄쨌(43). 10. The method of modular configurable architecture for protection against lightning (Figure 1), characterized in that, the electronic assemblies (30)횄쨌(43) are manufactured as a standalone devices or as a group of standalone and integrated devices, independent from the lightning arrester of modular configurable architecture (80) or from any other lightning arrester or system for protection against lightning (60).
